title: hieracium lanatum subsp. rupestre
intro:
Hieracium lanatum subsp. rupestre, also known as a mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieraclium lanatum plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, known for its distinctive appearance with hairy leaves and stems, and bright yellow daisy-like flowers.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium lanatum subsp. rupestre include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an immune system booster and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Hieracium lanatum subsp. rupestre is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
